wave_plan.name: retrofit raises across codebase to HarnessError subclasses
wave_plan.completion_note: Scope refined during execution: 4 raises retrofitted (1 in dpapi.py to ConfigCorruption, 3 in jsonl_log.py to SchemaViolation). Pydantic validators, user-input validation in loader, dispatcher's contract-protected raise (L81), and assertion-style RuntimeErrors intentionally left as ValueError/RuntimeError — they don't fit the L3-L5 operational error model. 89/89 tests still green.

wave_plan.name: canonical status tracker as harness primitive (roster #19)
wave_plan.note: Recovery layer for autonomous loops. Required before #20 because both touch cli.py.
wave_plan.completion_note: Kimi-CLI dispatch timed out at 1200s but landed 4/5 files incrementally per [[feedback_kimi_cli_incremental_edits]] (schema.py, store.py, hooks.py, __init__.py). Claude inline authored tests/test_status.py (42 tests), promoted cli.py status command to group with 7 subcommands (preserving legacy as 'status report'), wired dispatcher.py hooks (wave_id-gated, opt-in), fixed 3 bugs Kimi introduced (key-inversion in write_status, tempfile leak on os.replace failure, unable to read rows with unquoted commas), and fixed the existing STATUS.csv row 3 (WA-1) unquoted-comma data bug. Final: 305/305 tests pass (was 263; +42 new); 90% coverage on harness.status. Test 305 exceeds packet's >=285 acceptance criterion.

wave_plan.name: independent observer (roster #20) — the check on dev-manager authority
wave_plan.completion_note: Kimi-CLI dispatch timed out at 1200s but landed massively: 6 module files (state.py 96%, audit_prompt.py 85%, cycle.py 89%, flags.py 83%, scheduler.py 32%, __init__.py 100%; total 79% coverage) + tests/test_observer.py (22KB, 41 tests) + cli.py observer group (12 subcommands) + manager.md Step 0 protocol. Claude only had to remove the obsolete observer_tick stub test (the stub was correctly replaced by the new observer group) + refresh cli.py docstring. 346/346 tests pass. Second hybrid wave in this session — Kimi-CLI's incremental edits are reliable even when swarm reports timeout.
wave_plan.note: Round 2 — was sequential after #19 due to cli.py conflict.

engine_slots._doc: Each tick, the manager fills idle Kimi slots with queued work. DeepSeek stays idle unless explicitly needed (cost-per-API constraint).

kimi.session_pattern_2026_05_21: Three consecutive timeout-but-landed runs (#19 bcsacjnox, #20 bq1ydqn3l, Round 3 b8nt1z3rd). Kimi-CLI incremental edits land robustly even when xaxiu-swarm reports timeout. Decision: treat swarm timeout as 'check git diff first' not 'failed'.

kimi-api.note: Production-ready up to 18 concurrent per warehouse 2026-05-12-p0-quartet (3-key pool x 6); 6 is the conservative default

deepseek.note: DeepSeek costs per API call; only dispatch when task needs its strengths (V-file, math/schema, novel-feature drafting)

dispatch_learnings.insight: xaxiu-swarm backend agentic capability differs: 'kimi' (CLI) is agentic and can apply in-place edits via Edit/Write tools. 'kimi-api' and 'deepseek' are NOT agentic — they produce a single text response written to --deliverable path. Packets requesting in-place edits must target kimi CLI; packets to api backends must request FIND/REPLACE or full-file-contents output the integrating supervisor parses.
evidence: Wave A.6 on deepseek-v4-flash: exit 0, prose output, no files modified
Wave B on kimi-api: exit 0, test code in output but not written to disk
